figures.caption: Table 7. Results on large-scale models, LLaVA1.5-13B and Qwen3VL-30B.

Figure 1. Pruning visualization on LLaVA1.5-7B with different budgets. Our MI-Pruner consistently identifies and preserves the queried regions, whereas other methods partially miss relevant information. Tokens from top to bottom: 64, 128, 256.

Table 2. Performance comparison of different pruning methods on Qwen3VL-2B and -8B. ”Attention” refers to the sum of attention scores from other image tokens. MI-Pruner and MI-Attention achieve SOTA performance for both scales.
Figure 5. Pruning visualization on Qwen3VL series. Our method retains the semantic-relative patches regarding prompts adaptively.
Table 11. POPE performance comparison under 3 settings on Qwen3VL. We report accuracy (Acc), F1 scores and Yes-rate.

Figure 2. Overview. Previous methods prune tokens by attention scores from vision encoder or LLM decoder. Our MI-Pruner calculates Mutual Information between visual and textual embeddings in the projection space, achieving optimal performance with minimal latency.
Table 10. POPE performance comparison under 3 settings on LLaVA1.5-7B. We report accuracy (Acc), F1 scores and Yes-rate.
Table 1. Performance comparison of different pruning methods on LLaVA1.5-7B. Methods before - - - are attention-based. We use color to distinguish best and second best.
